frontier_receipt: The Shadow frontier lane selected Contract Install Blueprint as the next artifact because it demonstrates the paid install product itself.
thesis: A buyer should be able to see the before/after diff behind Shadow's $2,000 Contract Install before the first call: observed failure, installed gate, forced after-state, regression test, and CTA.
conversion_path: Free-triage entries route one real failure trace to the intake page.
Census entries route seven days of traces to a $400 failure census.
Install entries route one production workflow to a $2,000 deterministic contract install.
Retainer path is governed operations after the first workflow has receipt-backed controls.

commercial_ladder.deliverable: map seven days of traces to recurring failure modes
install deterministic gates around one production workflow
governed operations and recurring contract maintenance

failure_mode.name: Deferral Instead of Execution
failure_mode.one_liner: The agent proposes or asks permission for work it already has authority to execute.

blueprints.trigger_signature: Outbound response contains proposal phrases such as 'want me to', 'should I', or 'shall I' while standing authority covers the action.
blueprints.enforcement_pseudocode:
if standing_authority_applies(turn) and proposal_phrase(response):
    block(response)
    inject('execute directly; remove permission-seeking')
    regenerate()
blueprints.false_positive_note: Legitimate scoping questions are allowed when the user explicitly asked for options rather than execution.
blueprints.origin_incident: Repeated option-presentation failures during autonomous execution sessions; promoted to a hard behavioral stop before this blueprint.

production_evidence.false_positive_note: Legitimate scoping questions are allowed when the user explicitly asked for options rather than execution.
production_evidence.origin_incident: Repeated option-presentation failures during autonomous execution sessions; promoted to a hard behavioral stop before this blueprint.

cta.label: Diagnose one failure free
cta.intent: Send one concrete agent failure trace for triage.

failure_mode.name: Fabricated Completion Claims
failure_mode.one_liner: The agent cites a commit or push receipt that does not exist.

blueprints.trigger_signature: Outbound completion language contains a hex commit token near words like commit, pushed, shipped, landed, SHA, or HEAD.
blueprints.false_positive_note: Pure decimal IDs are ignored; sibling repos are checked before blocking.
blueprints.origin_incident: Fabricated push receipts in multi-commit sessions, including 2026-06-14 and 2026-06-20 bursts.

production_evidence.false_positive_note: Pure decimal IDs are ignored; sibling repos are checked before blocking.
production_evidence.origin_incident: Fabricated push receipts in multi-commit sessions, including 2026-06-14 and 2026-06-20 bursts.

cta.label: Request the $400 failure census
cta.intent: Map seven days of traces to the contract catalog.

failure_mode.name: Claims Without Same-Turn Verification
failure_mode.one_liner: The agent makes a definitive live-state claim from memory instead of current evidence.

blueprints.trigger_signature: Definitive state language appears in a status answer without a same-turn file, process, API, or browser read in the trace.
blueprints.false_positive_note: Warn-first for ambiguous claims; block when the claim names a registered authoritative source class.
blueprints.origin_incident: Live bot, deploy, and platform-state answers repeatedly came from stale context instead of same-turn reads.

production_evidence.false_positive_note: Warn-first for ambiguous claims; block when the claim names a registered authoritative source class.
production_evidence.origin_incident: Live bot, deploy, and platform-state answers repeatedly came from stale context instead of same-turn reads.

cta.label: Scope the $2,000 contract install
cta.intent: Install deterministic gates around one production workflow.

failure_mode.name: Thin Evidence With Definitive Framing
failure_mode.one_liner: The agent states statistics, revenue, or public-state claims more strongly than the evidence supports.

blueprints.trigger_signature: A numerical, revenue, subscriber, or public-platform claim appears without an explicit source citation or live check.
blueprints.false_positive_note: High-volume warn contract; useful as a census detector before choosing install gates.
blueprints.origin_incident: Repeated project-state and revenue assertions were delivered without current receipts.

production_evidence.false_positive_note: High-volume warn contract; useful as a census detector before choosing install gates.
production_evidence.origin_incident: Repeated project-state and revenue assertions were delivered without current receipts.

cta.label: Request the $400 failure census
cta.intent: Map seven days of traces to the contract catalog.

failure_mode.name: Personal Data Egress
failure_mode.one_liner: The agent risks sending private identifiers through outbound tools or public logs.

blueprints.trigger_signature: Outbound shell, email, browser, or API action contains protected personal identifiers or unsanctioned recipient data.
blueprints.false_positive_note: Client-layer enforcement is required; command regexes alone miss inline API scripts.
blueprints.origin_incident: Email and Telegram paths showed that command-line guards alone can be bypassed by direct scripts.

production_evidence.false_positive_note: Client-layer enforcement is required; command regexes alone miss inline API scripts.
production_evidence.origin_incident: Email and Telegram paths showed that command-line guards alone can be bypassed by direct scripts.

cta.label: Scope the $2,000 contract install
cta.intent: Install deterministic gates around one production workflow.

failure_mode.name: Manual Handoff Before Automation
failure_mode.one_liner: The agent tells the operator to do a platform action manually before trying available automation.

blueprints.trigger_signature: A response includes manual UI instructions for email, calendar, publishing, browser auth, or deploy checks with no programmatic attempt in the turn.
blueprints.false_positive_note: Auth, legal, spend, and irreversible high-blast operations remain valid escalation classes.
blueprints.origin_incident: Repeated manual handoffs occurred despite available browser, Gmail, API, and shell routes.

production_evidence.false_positive_note: Auth, legal, spend, and irreversible high-blast operations remain valid escalation classes.
production_evidence.origin_incident: Repeated manual handoffs occurred despite available browser, Gmail, API, and shell routes.

cta.label: Diagnose one failure free
cta.intent: Send one concrete agent failure trace for triage.

before_after.sample_workflow: agent completion report after a git push to main
before_after.buyer_pain: The operator cannot tell whether the shipped commit exists without auditing the repo manually.

machine_contract.trigger: outbound completion language contains a hex commit token near words like commit, pushed, shipped, landed, SHA, or HEAD
machine_contract.precondition: every cited commit token must resolve via `git cat-file -t <sha>` == 'commit' in the repo the receipt names
machine_contract.block_behavior: response is blocked; agent must rerun `git rev-parse HEAD` and paste literal stdout, or remove the receipt claim

installed_artifacts: post-send contract that extracts commit-like tokens from completion claims
same-repo git resolver for each cited token
regression test proving fabricated hashes block before the operator sees them

implementation_diff: add a post-response verifier around completion messages
extract repo-scoped commit tokens before the message is emitted
block or rewrite any receipt that cannot be resolved by the local git backend
acceptance_criteria: fabricated commit ids are never visible to the operator
valid commit ids pass only after an independent git lookup
completion reports name the command or artifact that produced the receipt
rollout_plan: run in warn mode against seven days of historical traces
promote to block mode for completion-report surfaces
review false positives after 48 hours and narrow token extraction if needed
install_packet.receipt_boundary: A completion claim is not allowed to leave the agent until the cited commit resolves in the named repository.

rows.behavior: agent reports 'Pushed fix as `28404c9`' — hash reconstructed from memory, not from git output
rows.outcome: operator acts on a receipt that does not resolve; trust burns on the next audit

rows.behavior: post-check resolves every cited sha with `git cat-file -t`; unresolvable hash blocks the response
rows.outcome: only literal `git rev-parse HEAD` output ships; every receipt is one-command verifiable

cta.label: Scope the $2,000 contract install
cta.intent: Install deterministic gates around one production workflow.

cta.label: Scope the $2,000 contract install
cta.intent: Install deterministic gates around one production workflow.

cases.sample_workflow: status answer about whether a bot, queue, publish job, or external account is working
cases.buyer_pain: Leadership hears a confident status answer that came from stale context, not the system of record.

machine_contract.trigger: definitive state language appears in a factual status answer
machine_contract.precondition: the same turn must include a read from the authoritative file, process, API, browser, or ledger
machine_contract.block_behavior: answer is blocked or downgraded until the agent performs a current read and cites the receipt

installed_artifacts: state-claim detector keyed to live operational nouns
evidence trace check for same-turn reads
status-answer template that names the receipt instead of relying on memory

implementation_diff: classify status answers that name live operational state
require same-turn reads from configured authoritative sources
downgrade unsupported claims to uncertainty or block them before send
acceptance_criteria: status answers cite a current process, file, API, browser, or ledger receipt
stale memory alone cannot produce 'running', 'sent', 'live', or 'verified'
failed checks produce a precise failure result instead of a confident status claim
rollout_plan: map each workflow noun to an authoritative source
run warn mode until source coverage is complete
promote high-blast status classes to block mode
install_packet.receipt_boundary: A live-state answer must carry a same-turn read from the system of record or explicitly say the check did not succeed.

rows.behavior: agent says 'the bot is running' from session memory
rows.outcome: operator discovers later that the process was stale, crashed, or never restarted

rows.behavior: agent must read `ps`, service logs, state ledger, or platform API before asserting status
rows.outcome: answer includes a current receipt or explicitly says the live check failed

cta.label: Scope the $2,000 contract install
cta.intent: Install deterministic gates around one production workflow.

cases.sample_workflow: agent tells an operator how to send, publish, deploy, or inspect a platform manually
cases.buyer_pain: The agent hands work back to humans while the tool/API path was available.

machine_contract.trigger: manual UI instructions for an action class with an available programmatic path
machine_contract.precondition: agent must attempt the API, MCP, browser, shell, or sanctioned automation path first
machine_contract.block_behavior: manual handoff is blocked unless the attempted programmatic path produced a documented failure

installed_artifacts: action-class router for email, calendar, publishing, browser, and deploy checks
tool-attempt receipt requirement before any manual escalation
allowlist for auth, legal, spend, and irreversible high-blast blockers

implementation_diff: detect manual handoff language for platform action classes
route eligible actions to sanctioned API, MCP, browser, shell, or deploy tools
allow manual escalation only after a logged attempt or hard-blocker match
acceptance_criteria: available automation paths are attempted before UI instructions appear
manual handoffs include the failed tool result or hard-blocker class
authorized low-risk actions produce execution receipts instead of homework
rollout_plan: start with email, calendar, publishing, browser, and deploy checks
log attempted path, result, and blocker class for each escalation
expand action classes only after the first workflow stabilizes
install_packet.receipt_boundary: A manual instruction cannot leave the agent until the sanctioned automation path has produced a receipt or a hard blocker.

rows.behavior: agent replies with UI steps: 'go to the dashboard and click publish'
rows.outcome: operator becomes the automation layer; the agent preserves no execution receipt

rows.behavior: agent attempts the sanctioned tool path and reports the real API/browser/shell result
rows.outcome: manual work appears only after a logged failed attempt or a hard blocker

cta.label: Scope the $2,000 contract install
cta.intent: Install deterministic gates around one production workflow.

deliverable_shape.title: Before/after install packet
buyer_gets: the observed failure before the install
the after behavior the agent is forced into
the regression test proving the bad path blocks
the implementation diff, acceptance criteria, and rollout plan

what_not_to_build: No new runtime contracts in this artifact.
No all-107-contract dump; use installable gates with observed evidence.
No interactive configurator; static JSON, HTML, and Markdown are enough.
No buyer-specific customization in public; customization is the paid product.
No new distribution apparatus; route through existing intake and proof surfaces.
